title: draba cinerea var. dovrensis
intro: Draba cinerea var. dovrensis, also known as Dovre Whitlowgrass, is a rare and endangered plant species belonging to the mustard family (Brassicaceae) and is native to Norway. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments. The health benefits of Dovre Whitlowgrass include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Dovre Whitlowgrass can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its extracts have also been used in folk medicine to treat coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues.
